title: Operation While Impaired (CK26041081)
content:
Operation While Impaired – CK26041081, CK26041163 and CK26041272
Date: June 7, 2026, 0357hrs    Location: Orangewood Boulevard, Chatham
On June 7, 2026, police responded to several incidents of impaired driving.
On June 7, 2026 at approximately 0357hrs, a vehicle was stopped on Orangewood Boulevard for erratic driving.
Police requested the driver provide a sample of his breath into an Approved Screening Device. After the device registered a “Fail”, the male was arrested and transported to the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters for further testing.
He was released unconditionally, however, his licence was suspended for seven days, as his Blood Alcohol Concentration was over 50mgs of alcohol in 100 millilitres of blood.
Also on June 7, 2026, at approximately 1252hrs, police responded to a single motor vehicle collision with a light pole on Fifth Street in Chatham. No injuries were reported.
Police requested the driver provide a sample of his breath into an Approved Screening Device. After the device registered a “Fail”, the male was arrested and transported to the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters for further testing, which revealed a Blood Alcohol Concentration over the legal limit.
As a result, a 32-year-old Chatham male is facing charges for Operation While Impaired – Blood Alcohol Concentration (80 Plus).
He was released on a Form 10 with a court date of July 13, 2026.
Later, on June 7, 2026 at approximately 2228hrs, an officer observed a vehicle swerving on McNaughton Avenue, failing to stop at stop signs and travelling at a high rate of speed.
While officers were attempting to stop the vehicle, the driver pulled into a Prince Albert Road driveway and fled inside a residence, freshly pursued by police.
The driver was arrested for Impaired Operation of a Conveyance and transported to the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters where he provided samples of this breath.
An 18-year-old Chatham male is facing charges for the following offences:
Operation While Impaired – Alcohol
Operation While Impaired – Blood Concentration 80-plus
He was released on a Form 10 with a court date of July 16, 2026.

title: Media Release: Community Collaboration Leads to Stunt Driving Charge
content:
The Chatham-Kent Police Service would like to highlight the important role that community members of Chatham-Kent play in helping to keep our roads safe.
On June 3, 2026, a concerned citizen contacted the Chatham-Kent Police Service regarding a white pickup truck reportedly travelling at excessive speed on Pioneer Line between St. Clair Road and Prince Albert Road during the early morning hours. Acting on the information provided, members of the Traffic Management Office conducted targeted speed enforcement in the area.
On June 4, 2026, at approximately 06:50 hrs, officers observed a vehicle matching the description provided by the community member travelling eastbound at a significant rate of speed. Officers confirmed the vehicle was travelling at 175 km/h in an 80 km/h zone. A traffic stop was conducted and a 23-year-old male from Pain Court was charged with:
Drive-Motor-Vehicle – Perform Stunt – Speed in excess of 50 km/h, contrary to section 172(1) of the Highway Traffic Act (HTA)
Speeding, contrary to section 128 of the Highway Traffic Act (HTA)
As a result, the vehicle was impounded for 14 days, and the driver’s license was suspended for 30 days under provincial administrative penalties.
The Chatham-Kent Police Service encourages residents to continue reporting traffic concerns. When residents and police work together, we can address dangerous driving behaviour and help make our roads safer for everyone.

title: Media Release – Pedestrian Struck by Vehicle at McNaughton Avenue East and Taylor Avenue
content:
On May 26, 2026, at 20:32 hrs., emergency crews were dispatched to a motor vehicle collision at the intersection of McNaughton Avenue East and Taylor Avenue. The incident involved a single vehicle and a lone pedestrian.
Emergency services arrived on the scene, where a 28-year-old female from Chatham sustained serious injuries. The driver, a 66-year-old female from Chatham, was not injured.
Due to the severity of the collision, the Traffic Management Unit was called in to handle the investigation, resulting in the temporary closure of the intersection at McNaughton Avenue and Taylor Avenue. The roadway has since been reopened.
This investigation remains open and ongoing. Anyone with further information regarding the collision is encouraged to contact the investigating officer, Constable Brandon McKeen, at
brandonmc@chatham-kent.ca
or 519-355-1092.

title: Media Release – CKPS Releases Results from Canada Road Safety Week
content:
The Chatham-Kent Police Service participated in Canada Road Safety Week from May 1 to May 18, 2026. This seven-day national campaign is dedicated to making Canada’s roads safer by encouraging responsible driving habits, saving lives, and reducing injuries.
The campaign focused on eliminating impaired, distracted, and aggressive driving as well as the failure to wear seatbelts.
Throughout the campaign, the Chatham-Kent Police Service laid a total of 479 Provincial Offence Notices, and 10 Criminal Code offences were issued to drivers across the municipality. In addition, 13 R.I.D.E. programs were conducted, stopping 1,478 vehicles.
The following charges and offences were issued during the campaign:
3 alcohol-impaired driving charges
1 drug-impaired driving charge
1 alcohol-refusal charge
5 seven-day licence suspensions
1 dangerous driving criminal charge
2 driving while prohibited criminal charges
5 electronic device offences
364 speeding offences
115 other provincial offences
The Chatham-Kent Police Service reminds all motorists to drive responsibly, obey traffic laws and to prioritize safety to protect lives on our roadways.

title: Media Release – CKPS Launches a Trespass Authority Agent Program for Business and Commercial Property Owners
content:
The Chatham-Kent Police Service is introducing the Trespass Authority Agent Program, a proactive initiative designed to help business and commercial property owners address ongoing trespassing and loitering.
The program allows registered business and commercial property owners to authorize the Chatham-Kent Police Service officers to act as their agent for the purpose of enforcing the Trespass to Property Act. This means that the Chatham-Kent Police Service may take enforcement action on behalf of the property owner without requiring the owner to be present at the time of an incident.
Property registration is important because under Ontario’s Trespass to Property Act, police cannot automatically act as an “agent” for every private property owner unless authority has been formally granted. A registration process creates a clear, documented relationship between the property owner and the police service, ensuring officers are acting lawfully and consistently when enforcing trespass issues. Registered properties will display Trespass to Property Act Agent signage identifying that police have pre-authorized authority to assist with trespass enforcement.
The program is designed to support property owners in maintaining a safe and secure environment. The Trespass Authority Agent Program applies to properties specifically enrolled in the program.
Business and commercial property owners are encouraged to register directly using the following link:
https://www.services.tryrubicon.com/chatham-kent-police-service/registries/select
The Chatham-Kent Police Service remains committed to working with property owners and community partners to enhance safety and security.

title: Media Release – Chatham-Kent Police Service Supports Canada Road Safety Week 2026
content:
The Chatham-Kent Police Service is proud to support Canada Road Safety Week that takes place from May 12–18, 2026.
Throughout the week, officers will participate in traffic enforcement initiatives and public awareness campaigns focused on keeping Chatham-Kent roads safe for all motorists, cyclists and pedestrians.
This national campaign, led by the Canadian Association of Chiefs of Police, focuses on reducing dangerous driving behaviours, such as distracted driving, impaired driving, speeding, aggressive driving and seatbelt violations.
Road safety remains a shared responsibility and the Chatham-Kent Police Service encourages all members of the community to make safe and responsible choices behind the wheel.
During Road Safety Week, the public can expect:
• Increased traffic enforcement throughout Chatham-Kent
• Daily road safety awareness and educational social media posts
• Traffic safety tips and reminders
• Community engagement initiatives promoting safe driving habits
Drivers are reminded to:
• Put away distractions while driving
• Obey posted speed limits
• Never drive impaired
• Wear seatbelts at all times
• Stay alert and share the road safely
“Our goal is simple, to help prevent collisions, injuries and fatalities on our roads. Every safe decision made behind the wheel can make a difference,” said Acting Sergeant Lisa Rodger.
The Chatham-Kent Police Service would like to thank the community for supporting road safety initiatives and helping keep Chatham-Kent roads safe for everyone.
Follow the Chatham-Kent Police Service on social media throughout the week for daily safety tips, awareness posts and community engagement content.

title: Media Release – Chatham-Kent Police Service responds to Fatal Train Incident
content:
On May 10, 2026, at approximately 14:08 hrs, Chatham-Kent Police Service responded to a report of a pedestrian struck by an eastbound passenger train in Chatham-Kent. The incident occurred on railway tracks in the area of Northwood Line. Despite emergency measures taken by the train operator, the individual, a 34-year-old male from Chatham, sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced deceased at the scene.
Officers secured the scene and the Traffic Management Unit was called in to investigate and coordinate with railway police and the Office of the Chief Coroner as part of the investigation. The rail corridor was temporarily closed while investigators conducted their work, and the train was later permitted to continue.
There is no ongoing risk to public safety. Out of respect for the family, no further details will be released.
Our thoughts are with the family and loved ones affected by this incident. On behalf of all members, we extend our deepest condolences during this incredibly difficult time, and we ask that the community respect the family’s privacy as they grieve.
If you or someone you know is struggling or in crisis, help is available 24/7. In Canada, call or text 9-8-8 for immediate, confidential support. In an emergency, call 9-1-1. Those with additional information surrounding this incident can contact the investigating officer Cst. Joel Rehill, at 519-355-1092 or by email at joelr@chatham-kent.ca

An 18-year-old Chatham-Kent female faces charges of:
Failure to Comply With Undertaking
Obstruct Police Officer
Facilitate Breach of Undertaking
Assault
Uttering Threats to Cause Death or Bodily Harm
A 26-year-old Chatham male faces charges of:
Failure to Comply With Undertaking
Facilitate Breach of Undertaking
Assault
Both were transported to the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters and held for bail.

title: Assault (IPV) (CK26040698)
content:
Assault (IPV) – CK26040698
Date: June 5, 2026, 2155hrs   Location: Wheatley
On June 5, 2026 at approximately 2155hrs, police were contacted regarding an assault on a female. The female sustained minor physical injuries after being pushed into a fence.
Prior to police arrival, the male left in a vehicle.
The Chatham-Kent Police Service notified the Ontario Provincial Police who located the male in their jurisdiction.
Essex OPP arrested the male for Impaired Operation of a Conveyance and Assault Peace Officer, before turning him over to Chatham-Kent Police.
A 45-year-old Wheatley man faces an additional charge of Assault.
He was transported to the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters and held for bail.
Media Contact:
ckpsmedia@chatham-kent.ca

title: Warrants (CK26040360)
content:
Warrants – CK26040360
Date: June 4, 2026, 17:11 hrs
Location: William Street and Park Street, Chatham, ON
On Thursday, June 4, 2026, at approximately 17:11 hrs, an officer of the Chatham-Kent Police Service was on general patrol in the area of William Street and Park Street. The officer observed a male party who was currently wanted with the Chatham-Kent Police.
The 32-year-old Chatham man was subsequently arrested on the strength of the warrant for:
Fail to comply with probation order
The accused was then released future court date of August 26, 2026.

title: Mischief (CK26040129)
content:
Mischief – CK26040129
Date: June 3, 2026, 2340hrs  Third Street, Chatham
On June 3, 2026, at approximately 2340hrs, a male entered the Chatham-Kent Police Service headquarters and proceeded to activate the fire alarm, despite there being no fire.
The male was arrested and proceeded to spit on a police officer after being lodged into cells. At the time of the incident, he was bound by a Probation Order, one of the conditions of which is to keep the peace and be of good behaviour.
As a result, a 31-year-old Chatham man faces the following charges:
False Alarm of Fire
Assault Peace Office
Fail to Comply With Probation Order
He was held for bail.
Media Contact:
ckpsmedia@chatham-kent.ca

title: Impaired/80+ (CK26039878)
content:
Impaired/80+ – CK26039878
Date: June 3, 2026, 02:33 hrs
Location: Claymore Line, Chatham Township, ON
On the early morning of Wednesday, June 3, 2026, at approximately 02:33 hrs, members of the Chatham-Kent Police Service located a vehicle on Claymore Line that was running and occupied.
While speaking with the individual officers formed grounds to believe the driver was impaired by alcohol, as a result an approved screening device demand was made to the driver. The driver refused the demand and was subsequently arrested for refusal.
As a result, a 21-year-old Port Lambton man was charged with:
Failure or refusal to comply with demand
The accused was then released with a future court date July 13, 2026

title: Intimate Partner Violence (IPV) (CK26039562)
content:
Intimate Partner Violence (IPV) – CK26039562
/
CK26039580
Date: June 1, 2026, 20:21 hrs
Location: Chatham, ON
On Monday, June 1, 2026, at approximately 20:21 hrs, members of the Chatham-Kent Police Service responded to disturbance to a residence in Chatham.
Through investigation police learned that two individuals had been involved in a physical altercation. Further investigation revealed that one individual had threatened the other with a knife.
As a result of the investigation, 24-year-old Chatham man was arrested and charged with:
Assault with a Weapon
Assault
He was transported to Chatham-Kent Police headquarters and later released with conditions and future court date of July 13, 2026.
A 19-year-old Chatham woman was also arrested and charged with:
Assault with a Weapon
Assault
She was transported to Chatham-Kent Police headquarters where she was held for bail.

title: Breach of Probation (CK26038357)
content:
Breach of Probation – CK26038357
Date: May 28, 2026, 23:35 hrs
Location: Raleigh Street, Chatham, ON,
On Thursday, May 28, 2026, at approximately 23:35 hrs, members of the Chatham-Kent Police Service responded to a residence in Chatham after receiving information that a male was breaching court-ordered probation conditions.
Through investigation, officers learned the accused was within prohibited distances of the victim’s residence, contrary to existing probation conditions. Police reviewed surveillance footage and conducted patrols in the area, locating the accused nearby.
As a result, a 29-year-old Chatham man was arrested and charged with:
Two counts of Fail to Comply with Probation Order
The accused was then transported to Chatham-Kent Police Service where he was held for bail.
Media Contact:
ckpsmedia@chatham-kent.ca

CK26042067 – Impaired driving
Date: June 10, 2026, 19:07 hrs
Location: First Street, Wallaceburg, ON
On Wednesday, June 10, 2026, at approximately 19:07 hrs, the Chatham-Kent Police Service received an impaired driving complaint on First Street in Wallaceburg. Police located the vehicle and conducted a traffic stop. During, which the driver was required to provide a roadside breath sample through an approved screening device, to which the driver subsequently failed.
The driver was arrested and brought to Chatham-Kent Police headquarters for further testing.
As a result of the investigation a 58-year-old Wallaceburg man has been charged with:
Operation while impaired – blood alcohol concentration (80 plus)
The accused was then released with a future court date of July 16, 2026.
